tool.name: EASTR
tool.description: emend alignments of spliced transcript reads by removing spurious splice junctions
tool.command:
## EASTR reads the reference FASTA (-r) and builds a Bowtie2 index and a .fai next ## to it, so symlink the FASTA to a writable name in the job directory. GTF/BED ## inputs are read directly (EASTR detects the input type from content, not ## extension). For BAM, symlink so the .bai index sits next to it and the ## filtered-BAM name is predictable. ln -s '$reference' reference.fa && #if $input_type.input_select == 'bam': ln -s '$input_type.input' input.bam && ln -s '$input_type.input.metadata.bam_index' input.bam.bai && #end if
eastr -r reference.fa -p "\${GALAXY_SLOTS:-1}" #if $input_type.input_select == 'bam': --bam input.bam --out_filtered_bam '$filtered_bam' #elif $input_type.input_select == 'gtf': --gtf '$input_type.input' #else: --bed '$input_type.input' #end if --out_removed_junctions '$removed_junctions' #if 'kept' in str($optional_outputs).split(','): --out_kept_junctions '$kept_junctions' #end if #if 'original' in str($optional_outputs).split(','): --out_original_junctions '$original_junctions' #end if --bt2_k $adv.bt2_k -o $adv.overhang -a $adv.anchor --min_duplicate_exon_length $adv.min_duplicate_exon_length --min_junc_score $adv.min_junc_score -A $adv.match_score -B $adv.mismatch_penalty -k $adv.kmer -w $adv.window -m $adv.min_chain_score #if $adv.trusted_bed: --trusted_bed '$adv.trusted_bed' #end if #if $adv.log: --verbose 2> '$log' #end if

param.name: input_select
param.label: Input type
param.help: EASTR can detect spurious splice junctions from a coordinate-sorted BAM of read alignments, or from junctions described in a GTF annotation or BED file. Only the BAM mode produces a filtered alignment file.

param.name: input
param.label: Aligned reads
param.help: Coordinate-sorted BAM file of spliced read alignments (e.g. from HISAT2 or STAR).

param.name: input
param.label: Transcript annotation
param.help: GTF file whose intron coordinates will be screened for spurious junctions.

param.name: input
param.label: Intron coordinates
param.help: BED file of intron/junction coordinates to screen for spurious junctions.

param.name: reference
param.label: Reference genome
param.help: Reference genome FASTA. EASTR uses it to extract intron-flanking sequences and builds a Bowtie2 index from it at runtime. For human data, provide a hg38 FASTA with the chrY pseudoautosomal regions (PARs) masked (see Help below).

param.label: Bowtie2 alignment threshold
param.help: Minimum number of distinct Bowtie2 alignments for a junction to be classified as spurious.

param.name: overhang
param.label: Overhang length
param.help: Length of flanking sequence taken on either side of a splice junction.

param.name: anchor
param.label: Minimum anchor length
param.help: Minimum required anchor length in each exon.

param.label: Minimum duplicate exon length
param.help: Minimum length for duplicated exon detection.

param.label: Minimum junction score
param.help: Minimum number of supporting reads per junction.

param.label: Trusted junctions
param.help: BED file of trusted junctions that will never be removed.

param.name: log
param.label: Generate log
param.help: Capture EASTR's verbose progress output (junctions found, spurious junctions detected, alignments removed) as a log dataset in your history.

tool.help:
**What it does**
EASTR (Emending Alignments of Spliced Transcript Reads) detects and removes spuriously spliced alignments from RNA-seq data. Splice-aware aligners such as HISAT2 and STAR can introduce erroneous spliced alignments between repeated sequences, which lead to falsely spliced transcripts in downstream assembly and quantification. EASTR examines the sequence similarity between the regions flanking an intron and the frequency of those sequences in the reference genome to identify junctions that are likely artifacts, independent of the aligner used.
**Inputs**
* **BAM** – a coordinate-sorted BAM file of read alignments. In this mode EASTR writes a *filtered BAM* with the spurious spliced alignments removed. * **GTF** – intron coordinates from a transcript annotation are screened. * **BED** – intron/junction coordinates are screened directly. * **Reference genome** – a FASTA file. EASTR uses it to extract intron-flanking sequences and builds a Bowtie2 index from it at runtime (Bowtie2 is provided by the package). Building the index is quick for small genomes but slower for whole genomes.
.. class:: warningmark
**Human data:** for hg38 we recommend providing a FASTA with the chrY pseudoautosomal regions (PARs) masked, to avoid spurious calls from the chrX/chrY PAR duplication. See the EASTR documentation for a PAR-masked reference and a script to build one.
**Outputs**
* **Removed (spurious) junctions** – a BED file of the junctions classified as artifacts (always produced). * **Filtered BAM** – the input alignments with spurious spliced reads removed (BAM input only). * **Kept junctions** / **Original junctions** – optional BED files of the retained and the pre-filter junction sets. * **Log** – an optional text log of EASTR's verbose progress output (enable *Generate log* under Advanced options).
**Citation**
Shinder I, Hu R, Ji HJ, Chao KH, Pertea M. EASTR: Identifying and eliminating systematic alignment errors in multi-exon genes. *Nature Communications*. 2023;14:7223.
